_meta.note: Generated by seed/build.ts from seed/calltree.seed.ts. Slack is a $45,000 (250-seat) deal; expectedValue = round(successProbability * 45000). Node win-rates are Beta-smoothed from 60 call outcomes.

nodes.title: Warm opening
nodes.description: Thanks for taking the time to chat today, Sarah — I’m excited to discuss how Slack can help your team.
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Discovery
nodes.description: Can you share how your team communicates and collaborates using Microsoft Teams right now?
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Incumbent objection
nodes.description: We already use Microsoft Teams — it's bundled with our license.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Reframe
nodes.description: Actually, Slack can run alongside Teams; it’s designed to complement your existing tools, not replace them.
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Curious buyer
nodes.description: Okay, but where does Slack actually win over Teams?
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Pilot offer
nodes.description: How about we set up a low-risk 2-week pilot with one of your teams to see how it goes?
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Pilot agreement
nodes.description: Sure, let’s run the pilot and see how it works out.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Pushback
nodes.description: We just standardized on Teams — not now.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Discovery-first
nodes.description: What aspects of Teams do you find most challenging in your day-to-day operations?
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Admitting pain
nodes.description: Honestly, the search functionality is weak and threads often get lost.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Show solution
nodes.description: Slack’s powerful search and organized threads can really address those pain points — would you like to see a demo?
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Demo booked
nodes.description: Yes, let’s schedule a demo to explore this further.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Disparaging Teams
nodes.description: I think Teams is a bit clunky and outdated — it really struggles with user experience.
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Defensive response
nodes.description: Just send me some info, please.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Cost inquiry
nodes.description: What does this run for 250 people?
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Value anchoring
nodes.description: Let’s focus on the value — think about the time saved per seat rather than just the sticker price.
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Proof request
nodes.description: Can you show me proof that it pays off at our size?
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Case study success
nodes.description: That case study is really relevant — I’m ready to move forward.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Price balking
nodes.description: I’m still not sure about the price.
nodes.speaker: buyer

nodes.title: Discount lead
nodes.description: We can offer you a discount to make this more appealing.
nodes.speaker: seller

nodes.title: Anchoring low
nodes.description: I was hoping for something even lower than that.
nodes.speaker: buyer

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Can you share how your team communicates and collaborates using Microsoft Teams right now?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: We already use Microsoft Teams — it's bundled with our license.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: What aspects of Teams do you find most challenging in your day-to-day operations?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Honestly, the search functionality is weak and threads often get lost.

transcript.speaker: seller

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Yes, let’s schedule a demo to explore this further.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Thanks for taking the time to chat today, Sarah — I’m excited to discuss how Slack can help your team.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Of course, happy to chat. We’re always looking for ways to improve our processes.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Great! Can you share how your team communicates and collaborates using Microsoft Teams right now?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Well, we primarily use Teams for messaging and video calls. It’s integrated with our other Microsoft tools, so it works for us.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I see. But have you found any challenges with it? Sometimes I hear that it can be a bit cumbersome.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I mean, it has its quirks, but it’s part of our Microsoft license, so it’s convenient.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I think Teams is a bit clunky and outdated — it really struggles with user experience. Slack is designed to be intuitive and user-friendly.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I wouldn’t say it’s outdated. It’s just different. We’ve adapted to it.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: But with Slack, you get a more streamlined experience. You can easily integrate with other apps and customize your workspace.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: I understand that, but we’ve already invested in Teams and it’s working for us.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I get that, but imagine how much more efficient your team could be with a tool that doesn’t feel like a chore to use.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Look, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but just send me some info, please.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Sure, I can do that. I just think you might be missing out on a better solution.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Thanks, but I really just want the information. I’ll review it.

aiNotes.triggerText: We already use Microsoft Teams — it's bundled with our license.
aiNotes.response: That's great to hear! Many of our customers use both Slack and Teams together. Slack excels in search capabilities, threaded conversations, and integrations with other tools, which can enhance your team's collaboration experience alongside Teams. Would you be open to exploring how Slack can complement your existing setup?

aiFeedback.summary: The rep's disparaging comments about Microsoft Teams immediately after the buyer mentioned it as their current solution caused the buyer to become defensive, leading to a loss of interest in continuing the conversation. This reaction ultimately derailed the opportunity to further explore Slack's value proposition.
strengths: Effective discovery question to understand the buyer's current situation.
weaknesses: Disparaging remarks about the incumbent solution alienated the buyer.
Failed to pivot the conversation positively after the incumbent objection.

practiceTargets.reason: Disparaging the incumbent made the buyer defensive and closed off further discussion.
practiceTargets.drill: Practice reframing negative comments into constructive comparisons or questions that invite dialogue.

practiceTargets.reason: This was a critical moment to acknowledge the buyer's current solution and position Slack as a complementary option.
practiceTargets.drill: Practice acknowledging the incumbent's strengths while highlighting Slack's unique benefits.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Thanks for taking the time to chat today, Sarah — I’m excited to discuss how Slack can help your team.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: Can you share how your team communicates and collaborates using Microsoft Teams right now?

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: We already use Microsoft Teams — it's bundled with our license.

transcript.speaker: seller
transcript.text: I think Teams is a bit clunky and outdated — it really struggles with user experience.

transcript.speaker: buyer
transcript.text: Just send me some info, please.
